title: ci: fix Zizmor-identified issues in workflows
body:
Fix various potentially security-relevant issues identified by [Zizmor](https://zizmor.sh/):

* Pin third-party action dependencies by commit hashes: https://docs.zizmor.sh/audits/#unpinned-uses
* Specify workflow permissions per job: https://docs.zizmor.sh/audits/#excessive-permissions
* Specify `persist-credentials` for `actions/checkout`: https://docs.zizmor.sh/audits/#artipacked
* Use environment variables to avoid template expansion in code contexts, which can lead to code injection vulnerabilities: https://docs.zizmor.sh/audits/#template-injection
* Use `cache: false` with `actions/setup-go` to prevent possible cache poisoning: https://docs.zizmor.sh/audits/#cache-poisoning
* Pass secrets explicitly to reusable workflow: https://docs.zizmor.sh/audits/#secrets-inherit

Also, a couple shell script issues fixed:

* Double-quote variable expansions to avoid unintended shell splitting and globbing: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC2086
* Use `--sandbox` for `sed` commands with dynamically generated scripts: https://www.gnu.org/software/sed/manual/sed.html#Command_002dLine-Options ("Sandbox mode ensures `sed` operates only on the input files designated on the command line, and cannot run external programs.")

#### Checklist

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

PR has been split into multiple commits, each addressing just one issue. (Let me know if it'd be better to also make this multiple PRs—that'd be easy enough to do now that I've split the commits.)

Thanks for the PR @HastD . I just checked out your branch locally and tried zizmor v1.18.0 installed via pip on Fedora 43 and got this:

```
$ zizmor .github/workflows/*
🌈 zizmor v1.18.0
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/check_cirrus_cron.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/dev-bump.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/first_contrib_cert_generator.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/issue-labeler.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/issue_pr_lock.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/labeler.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/mac-pkg.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/machine-os-pr.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/needs-info-labeler.yaml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/release-artifacts.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/release.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/stale.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/update-podmanio.yml
 INFO audit: zizmor: 🌈 completed .github/workflows/upload-win-installer.yml
error[dangerous-triggers]: use of fundamentally insecure workflow trigger
  --> .github/workflows/first_contrib_cert_generator.yml:5:1
   |
 5 | / on:
 6 | |   pull_request_target:
 7 | |     types: [closed]
 8 | |     branches:
...  |
16 | |         description: 'The pull request number'
17 | |         required: true
   | |______________________^ pull_request_target is almost always used insecurely
   |
   = note: audit confidence → Medium

error[dangerous-triggers]: use of fundamentally insecure workflow trigger
 --> .github/workflows/labeler.yml:3:1
  |
3 | / on:
4 | | - pull_request_target
  | |_____________________^ pull_request_target is almost always used insecurely
  |
  = note: audit confidence → Medium

error[dangerous-triggers]: use of fundamentally insecure workflow trigger
 --> .github/workflows/machine-os-pr.yml:3:1
  |
3 | / on:
4 | |   pull_request_target:
5 | |     paths:
6 | |       - 'version/rawversion/version.go'
  | |_______________________________________^ pull_request_target is almost always used insecurely
  |
  = note: audit confidence → Medium

45 findings (42 suppressed): 0 informational, 0 low, 0 medium, 3 high

```

Is that expected?

Also, @containers/podman-maintainers , I think it'd be nice to have zizmor run as a separate CI job or part of validate.

@lsm5 Yes, this PR doesn't fix that issue—replacing `pull_request_target` workflow triggers sometimes requires more significant refactoring and I was trying to limit the scope of this PR to addressing the issues that could be fixed more straightforwardly with minimal risk of breaking anything in CI. I'll go ahead and open an issue about the use of insecure workflow triggers so we have a place to discuss it.

I also agree it'd be a good idea to add a Zizmor workflow to flag workflow issues if future PRs reintroduce them. It's easy to set up, there's a GitHub action: https://docs.zizmor.sh/integrations/#via-zizmorcorezizmor-action
